title: Success State UIs for Perfect Scores

requirements.title: No dangerouslySetInnerHTML in new components
requirements.mitigation: All LLM-generated content (skill names, scores, summaries) must be rendered via standard React JSX text interpolation, never via dangerouslySetInnerHTML.

requirements.title: Share CTA must not expose session-bound URLs
requirements.threat: T-2: Session ID exposure via share functionality
requirements.mitigation: The 'Share your results' CTA should share only a generic congratulatory message without session IDs, report URLs, or user-specific assessment data. Use navigator.share() or navigator.clipboard.writeText() with a static message template.

checks.description: Share handler does not include sessionId in shared content
checks.verify: Shared text/URL does not contain session UUID or /api/ paths

checks.description: No report endpoint URL in clipboard/share payload

requirements.title: CTA navigation uses hardcoded internal paths only
requirements.threat: T-3: Open redirect via CTA navigation
requirements.mitigation: All CTA click handlers must use router.push() with hardcoded string paths or paths derived from trusted application state. No user-supplied input (query params, form fields) should control navigation targets.

validation.verify: All router.push() calls use string literals or application state paths, no external/user-controlled URLs

requirements.title: No new dependencies with known vulnerabilities
requirements.mitigation: No new npm dependencies should be required. If any are added, they must be vetted via npm audit.

existingControls.description: Both pages redirect unauthenticated users to /login. New success-state branches render within these pages and inherit the guard.
React escapes all text content in JSX expressions by default. No dangerouslySetInnerHTML usage exists in the codebase.
Backend CORS middleware restricts allowed origins via settings.cors_origins (not wildcard).
GapItem, GapAnalysis, ProficiencyScore types are auto-generated from backend Pydantic models, ensuring type-safe data handling.
